# Cue Clock

Cue Clock is a minimal, distraction-free clock and countdown timer app designed specifically for broadcast professionals who need to monitor multiple timezones and track countdown timers simultaneously.

## Key Information
- **Website**: https://cueclock.app
- **Web App**: https://live.cueclock.app
- **Repository**: https://github.com/yanukadeneth99/Cue-Clock
- **Developer**: YASHURA (https://yashura.io)
- **License**: AGPL-3.0 (Commercial licensing available)
- **Cost**: Completely Free (No Ads, No Subscriptions)
- **Privacy Policy**: https://cueclock.app/privacy

## Features
- **Dual Live Clocks**: Side-by-side clocks configurable to 18 broadcast timezones.
- **Multiple Countdown Timers**: Infinite timers tied to specific zones with persistent state.
- **Deduction Offsets**: Subtract pre-show buffer durations for setup and prep.
- **On-Air (Full-Screen) Mode**: Distraction-free, full-screen display for studio environments.
- **Persistent State**: All settings, zones, and timers saved locally across sessions via AsyncStorage.
- **Per-Timer Alerts**: Configurable minutes-before push notifications and in-app alerts.
- **In-App Help**: Integrated guide explaining all controls and usage patterns.

## Tech Stack
- **Web App**: React, Tailwind CSS, GSAP.
- **Mobile App**: React Native (Expo), Luxon for timezone handling, expo-notifications.
- **Website**: Next.js 16.2.1, Tailwind CSS 4, GSAP animations.
- **State Management**: AsyncStorage (local persistence), no cloud dependencies.

## Mission
Built to provide a reliable, straightforward, and simple timing tool for broadcast professionals during live production. Completely free and open-source with no enterprise complexity or hidden costs.
